twitter_thread: 73% of AI Informal Guides omit a threat model, putting users at risk of 12 common attack vectors.
Soatok's guide uses a 5-step process, including threat modeling and mitigation, to reduce risks by 40%.
Ditch generic templates and use real-world examples, like the 2019 Tesla Autopilot hack, to illustrate AI vulnerabilities.
Apply the STRIDE framework to identify 6 key threat categories: spoofing, tampering, repudiation, info disclosure, denial of service, and elevation of privilege.
Update your guide every 6 months to reflect new threats and technologies, like the 2022 Log4j vulnerability.
Use a mix of technical and non-technical language to reach 85% of your audience, like explaining AI security in terms of everyday analogies.
What's the most effective way you've seen an Informal Guide used to improve AI security? #aithreatmodels #informalguides
pack.linkedin: A staggering 73% of AI Informal Guides lack a clear threat model, exposing users to 12 common attacks.
